title: Living in Boulder Vlog 2 - 24 - 2017: Strava in Background

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog from Boulder, discussing a failed upload the previous night (no sound due to a computer glitch), his ongoing work on a free audiobook website with thousands of pages of book content, and a vision for a large-scale page template that could hold up to a million pages of books. He also mentions his car is ready for pickup but he's waiting until Monday.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Was too tired to redo the video at 2am, blames a computer glitch not user error
Excited about building out a large audiobook/book content website with elaborate page structures
Enthusiastic about an ambitious vision for a hierarchical book template system
Deliberately waiting until Monday rather than picking it up over the weekend

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't upload the video yesterday because there was no sound, possibly due to making it extra wide to show the side.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll use the same background for the video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The background I used last night was strong, but my computer crashed while working on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was going to do a print around on Boulder, showing different styles of sections by clicking on different folders.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have different sports categories like running, mountain biking, road biking, cycle across, and mountain climbing, but I don't have all of them now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'll do a video about the sports categories later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday, I was working on a good notebook and free audio history books for my website.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't realize how bad my website was, but I made it look pretty and will fix the links later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today, I'm working on the history and education page, and then the Google page, just adding words.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have almost 500,000 pages of books and audiobooks on my list between Kildo and Malibu Luxe.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My website has over a thousand pages just on the chapter contents that I made.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently figuring out how many pages the website actually has.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm tying everything up, and it's going to be fun and quick.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday, the sound didn't upload, possibly due to my computer acting weird.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was going to cancel the upload because I found out it was an effort, but I was too tired to redo it at 2 in the morning.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm not as tired now and will work more on some type of videos through Google.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought of another cool page template for books, with five columns across, each column having a chapter with 100 pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The template could be used for a 1000-page book, with each row representing 10 chapters, totaling 100,000 pages.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I envision a million-page book with a billion characters, but I'll explain it later.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to redo the video yesterday, but Premiere crashed, and I lost the project.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My computer is slow, causing issues like not seeing myself while recording and taking an hour to render.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The lesson from last night was to restart the computer, but it's still slow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The sound issue might have been a glitch, not something I did, because I tested the sound.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My car is ready, but I didn't pick it up because it's slow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to work on my voice over the weekend and focus on other tasks on Monday.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Calling about the car will help me look cooler.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The video I made is just about six minutes long, but I forgot to check the exact time.
